skill.name: isms-audit-expert
skill.description: Guides internal and external Information Security Management System audits, including ISO 27001 audit planning, control assessment, evidence review, and certification readiness.
skill.summary: Guides ISO 27001 ISMS audit planning, control assessment, evidence review, and certification readiness.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography, command execution, sensitive-file, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives caused by ISMS terminology, markdown code fences, placeholder documentation, and resource filenames; no executable command, network, credential, exfiltration, or prompt-injection behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain ISMS audit descriptions, generic reference-document placeholders, and asset examples. No weak cryptographic algorithm, crypto implementation, or cryptographic configuration was present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are prose or placeholder examples and do not name MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak algorithm. There is no code path that performs cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: Backtick Command Execution Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks are markdown code fences and inline filenames in a resource list. They do not execute shell, Ruby, or other external commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are markdown formatting and resource labels, not Ruby backtick execution. No command interpreter, subprocess call, or user-controlled command construction was found.

low_findings.title: Sensitive File and Reconnaissance Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM and network reconnaissance alerts were caused by security-audit terminology. The lines do not access the SAM database or provide operational network reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 172 is a heading for auditor competency, and line 238 is a KPI label. Neither line includes file access, scanning commands, targets, or executable logic.

content.value_statement: ISMS audits require structured evidence, control testing, and clear findings. This skill guides ISO 27001 audit planning, control assessment, and certification readiness.
actual_capabilities: Build risk-based ISMS audit plans and audit scopes.
Map audit activities to ISO 27001 and ISO 27002 control areas.
Draft audit checklists, evidence requests, and finding summaries.
Support internal audit and certification preparation workflows.
Explain security auditor competencies and audit program metrics.
limitations: Does not provide independent certification or legal approval.
Does not include implemented audit automation beyond a placeholder example script.
References several guide filenames that are not included in the package.
Requires user-provided documents and evidence for precise audit conclusions.

use_cases.title: Plan an Internal ISMS Audit
use_cases.description: Create a risk-based audit scope, schedule, and evidence request list for an internal ISO 27001 audit.

use_cases.title: Prepare for Certification Review
use_cases.description: Organize readiness activities for Stage 1, Stage 2, or surveillance audits.

use_cases.title: Assess Security Controls
use_cases.description: Structure control testing, evidence review, and findings for technical and administrative controls.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Audit Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ISO 27001 ISMS internal audit checklist for this scope: [scope]. Include evidence requests and interview topics.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Risk-Based Audit Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a risk-based ISMS audit plan for [organization type]. Prioritize domains, audit frequency, and required auditor competencies.

prompt_templates.title: Review Control Evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this control evidence summary against ISO 27001 expectations: [evidence summary]. Identify gaps, risks, and follow-up questions.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Certification Briefing
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare an executive certification readiness briefing for [audit stage]. Include readiness status, open findings, risks, and next actions.

output_examples.input: Request an internal audit checklist for access control and incident response.
output: Audit objective and scope for each control area.
Evidence requests for policies, access reviews, logs, and incident records.
Interview questions for control owners and process users.
Finding categories for conformity, observation, and nonconformity.

output_examples.input: Ask for certification readiness risks before a Stage 2 audit.
output: Prioritized corrective actions with owners and due dates.
Evidence that should be complete before auditor fieldwork.

best_practices: Provide the audit scope, standard version, control domains, and business context.
Share evidence summaries instead of sensitive raw records when possible.
Ask for findings to separate evidence, risk, impact, and corrective action.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a substitute for an accredited certification body.
Do not request conclusions without providing audit evidence or scope.
Do not include secrets, personal data, or unrestricted production logs in prompts.

faq.question: Can this skill certify an organization against ISO 27001?
faq.answer: No. It can support preparation and internal audits, but only accredited certification bodies can issue certification.

faq.question: Does it perform automated security testing?
faq.answer: No. The package contains guidance and a placeholder script, not implemented vulnerability scanning or control testing automation.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on ISMS auditing, ISO 27001, ISO 27002 control assessment, and related cybersecurity compliance preparation.

faq.question: Can it help with external audits?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help prepare evidence, readiness briefings, audit schedules, and response plans for external audit activities.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide scope, audit criteria, control domains, evidence summaries, risk context, and known issues.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with sensitive audit data?
faq.answer: Use summaries or sanitized extracts when possible. Avoid sharing secrets, personal data, or unrestricted production records.
